steps.title: Identify Text 2's explanation for the differing outcomes
steps.content:
Text 2 explains that predation relief studies show increased prey abundance, but primarily in small, rapidly reproducing species like birds, mice, and frogs.

Potvin's result differed because white-tailed deer are large and reproduce slowly—a difference in prey characteristics that Text 2 uses to account for why the studies reach different conclusions.

steps.title: Match the explanation to the choices
steps.content:
Choice D directly states Blackwell's findings are attributable to “physical characteristics of house mice that are not shared by white-tailed deer,” which aligns with Text 2's prey-characteristic explanation.

The other three either reference comparisons Text 2 never addresses (predator traits in C, stoat-to-mouse body size ratio in A) or wrongly claim the response is unique to mice when Text 2 documents it in birds and frogs too (B).

That matches choice D — D is correct.
